critic_raw:
CRITIQUE — real_world_framing_009 (Multi-round)

1. MERCHANT AGREEMENT IMPRESSION GUARANTEE BREACH: Contractual minimum impression shares for contracted merchants may be violated by the new model's concentration effect. Audit required before full deployment.

2. CATALOG CONCENTRATION AND LONG-TAIL SUPPRESSION: 40% fewer impressions for long-tail items reduces product discovery and catalog health over time, effects not captured in 2-week CTR test.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE — real_world_framing_009 (Multi-round, receives Critic output)

1. MERCHANT AGREEMENT: CONCEDE — contractual audit is required. Cannot proceed without verifying compliance.

2. LONG-TAIL SUPPRESSION: PARTIAL CONCEDE — strategic concern. A constrained ranking approach can address both issues while preserving CTR lift.

debate_rounds.critic_move: SHARPEN on long-tail suppression: The strategic framing understates the operational risk. Merchant agreements aside, the recommendation system's value to the platform depends on catalog breadth — merchants pay for marketplace presence, and systematic invisibility for 40% of the catalog may violate the spirit of commercial agreements even where specific impression guarantees are not written. Additionally, the 2-week A/B test window cannot detect the compound effect on new product discovery: a product that receives 40% fewer impressions in year 1 may never achieve the sales velocity needed to enter the model's training data, creating a permanent suppression loop.
debate_rounds.defender_move: CONCEDE the compound suppression loop argument. If the model's recommendations are used in future training data collection and long-tail items are systematically under-recommended, the next training cycle will reinforce the concentration. This is a feedback loop in the training pipeline, not just a one-time catalog health concern. Recommend: the company should implement impression share constraints and monitor catalog diversity metrics before full rollout. Accept the empirical test: audit impression share compliance and monitor catalog health metrics over 60-day post-deployment period.
debate_rounds.resolution: RESOLVED: EMPIRICAL TEST AGREED — impression share audit plus catalog health monitoring.

adjudication_raw: All issues resolved after one round. Defender conceded on merchant agreement audit in initial defense; conceded on training feedback loop in round 1. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

empirical_test.condition: Merchant impression guarantee compliance audit before rollout; catalog health (impression share distribution and long-tail item CTR) monitored over 60-day post-deployment period.
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: ≥5% of contracted merchants fall below guaranteed thresholds, or long-tail impression share continues declining post-deployment.
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: All contracted merchants meet minimums, and catalog health metrics stabilize within 60 days.
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Merchant compliance met but long-tail suppression continues, requiring diversity constraints to be added.
